name: Paystack Initialization Tests
primary_area: Payments / QA / Security / Idempotency / Boundary Tests
implementation_code_allowed: tests_first; minimal production patches only to satisfy VS-06B contract
forbidden_changes: Paystack webhook controller, route, or worker
Redis inventory reserve, consume, release, or expiry mutation
Production secrets in fixtures or logs

red_requires_failure_when: Valid active checkout/order cannot initialize one Paystack transaction
Invalid order or checkout state calls Paystack
Missing buyer_email calls Paystack without approved fallback policy
Amount, currency, or provider_reference are accepted from client input
Duplicate initialization creates duplicate active PaymentAttempts or duplicate provider calls
Provider timeout/error raises raw exceptions or marks order paid
Missing/invalid Paystack config leaks secrets or calls provider
Authorization URL, access_code, raw response, headers, email, phone, secret keys, or tokens appear in logs
PaymentEvent/webhook/verification/ticketing/scanner/WhatsApp/Redis inventory behavior appears in this slice
green_requires: Happy path creates one safe PaymentAttempt/link
Invalid states return safe errors and do not call provider
Duplicate calls return the existing active attempt/link
Provider/config failures are normalized and do not mutate paid/ticket/inventory states
StateTransition audit exists for allowed state changes

performance_and_scaling.hot_data: None introduced; Redis inventory keys must not be mutated
performance_and_scaling.ttl_strategy: Only bounded Redis SETNX lock TTL if VS-06B uses a short provider-init lock; no inventory TTL changes
performance_and_scaling.pubsub: None unless VS-06B already defined a payment-link notification

security_impact: Strengthens tests around provider secrets, access_code, authorization_url, raw responses, PII, and safe error handling.
